museo_del_prado.description: Spain's flagship picture gallery: Velázquez, Goya, Titian, and Rubens in a sober neo-classical shell.
museo_del_prado.history: Royal collection opened as public museum in 1819; extensions and reordering continued into the 21st century.
museo_del_prado.significance: Free windows and blockbuster loans; expect timed entries at peaks.
facts: Evening openings run on select days—check the official calendar.
The Jerónimos wing connects to historic cloister views.

royal_palace_madrid.description: Bourbon ceremonial palace with Plaza de Armas courtyard and interiors rivalling Versailles in scale.
royal_palace_madrid.history: Built on the burned Alcázar site from the 1730s onward by Italian and Spanish architects.
royal_palace_madrid.significance: Working royal venue; many rooms open on the visitor circuit.
facts: Changing of the guard happens on fixed mornings.
Combine with Almudena façade across the elevation.

plaza_mayor_madrid.description: Arcaded 17th-century plaza—Philip III on horseback, cafés with umbrellas, and holiday markets.
plaza_mayor_madrid.history: Habsburg urban theatre for autos-da-fé, bullfights, and civic ritual; Casa de la Panadería anchors the north.
plaza_mayor_madrid.significance: Controlled symmetry ideal for first light before tour groups.
facts: Pickpockets work the Christmas stalls—mind backpacks.
Side alleys lead toward La Latina tapas strips.

puerta_del_sol.description: Radial hub with clock tower, Kilometre Zero plaque, and New Year's grape-swallowing ritual on TV.
puerta_del_sol.history: Former city gate footprint became democratic protest and celebration stage.
puerta_del_sol.significance: Metro convergence and Tío Pepe neon in the night skyline.
facts: Bear-and-strawberry-tree statue marks the city symbol.
Expect dense crowds at any hour—cross walks carefully.

retiro_crystal_palace.description: Iron-and-glass pavilion beside a small lake—Victorian engineering transplanted to Madrid gardening.
retiro_crystal_palace.history: Built for the 1887 Philippines Exposition; now hosts temporary art installs.
retiro_crystal_palace.significance: Rowboats and shade trees make the basin a summer refuge.
facts: Palacio de Velázquez sits deeper in the park for more exhibits.
Weekend drum circles gather near the main fountain axis.

madrid_atocha.description: Tropical garden under wrought-iron vaults—high-speed hub linking Andalusia and the Mediterranean.
madrid_atocha.history: Historic terminal expanded; 1992 glass hall by Rafael Moneo inserted alongside platforms.
madrid_atocha.significance: Sculpture garden atmosphere while waiting for AVE departures.
facts: Separate signage for commuter Cercanías and long-distance tracks.
Souvenir shops cluster toward the newer extension.

las_ventas.description: Mudéjar-trimmed bullring—love or loathe corridas, the civic symbolism is undeniable.
las_ventas.history: 1929–1934 construction; still hosts major San Isidro season corridas.
las_ventas.significance: Guided visits explain sand geometry and tiers when no fight runs.
facts: Ethical visitors may prefer architecture tours to corridas.
Metro connects faster than traffic on festival days.

fountain_cibeles.description: Chariot goddess and lions framing the Cybele Palace—Real Madrid victory celebrations soak it in foam.
fountain_cibeles.history: 1782 fountain by Ventura Rodríguez; civic iconography merged with football lore.
fountain_cibeles.significance: Traffic rotary photography needs patience and wide lens.
facts: Bank of Spain neighbours the fountain's eastern flank.
Night floodlighting dramatises marble surfaces.

puerta_de_alcala.description: Triple-arched triumphal gate predating Parisian models—granite clad in evening colour washes.
puerta_de_alcala.history: Finished 1778 under Charles III as eastern city marker.
puerta_de_alcala.significance: Axis link between Retiro walks and Salamanca streets.
facts: Traffic circles the monument—use underpass crossings.
Pride lighting occasionally themes the stonework.

gran_via_madrid.description: Theatre façades, rooftop bars, and belle-époque neon stitched into a busy canyon.
gran_via_madrid.history: Early 20th-century slash through historic fabric to relieve congestion.
gran_via_madrid.significance: Nightlife density from plaza de España toward Callao.
facts: Look up for art-deco caps Metropolis and Telefónica.
Film première crowds still treat Capitol cinema as ritual.

almudena_cathedral.description: Modern neo-Romanesque and neo-Gothic cathedral consecrated in 1993 after a long hiatus.
almudena_cathedral.history: Plans dragged from the 16th century through civil wars and Franco era.
almudena_cathedral.significance: Crypt tours and colourful interior ceilings surprise first-time visitors.
facts: Wedding of crown prince raised its profile in 2004.
Museum section explains architectural phases.

templo_de_debod.description: Real Egyptian Ptolemaic temple reassembled on a reflecting pool—gift after the Aswan dam salvage.
templo_de_debod.history: Stones arrived 1968; alignment celebrates Spanish–Egyptian cooperation.
templo_de_debod.significance: Sunset silhouettes across the water are wildly popular.
facts: Free entry with bag checks at peak sunset.
Respect quiet—this is a small archaeological enclosure.

museo_thyssen.description: Old Masters to Pop in a palatial villa—completes the triad with Prado and Reina Sofía.
museo_thyssen.history: Baron Thyssen-Bornemisza collection housed here since the early 1990s.
museo_thyssen.significance: Strong on German Expressionism and American scene painting.
facts: Joint tickets exist with sister museums—compare bundles.
Late Friday hours help dodge tour-bus timing.

museo_reina_sofia.description: Spain's modern-art citadel—Guernica anchors rooms of Miró, Dalí, and contemporary installation.
museo_reina_sofia.history: 18th-century hospital buildings adapted by Jean Nouvel and others.
museo_reina_sofia.significance: Free windows matter for students; crowds cluster at Picasso's masterpiece.
facts: Carrier backpacks must often be left in lockers.
Sabatini building wings surround a courtyard escalator drama.

mercado_san_miguel.description: Iron market hall turned gourmet pincho counters—tourist premiums but high sensory payoff.
mercado_san_miguel.history: 1916 structure renovated for upscale grazing in the 2000s.
mercado_san_miguel.significance: Quick introduction to jamón grades and vermouth culture.
facts: Standing-room etiquette prevails at peak lunch.
Compare prices with traditional bars one street out.

plaza_de_espana_madrid.description: Wide square with Cervantes memorial and bronze Don Quixote tipping toward Sancho.
plaza_de_espana_madrid.history: Designed in the first third of the 20th century; skyscrapers now flank the horizon.
plaza_de_espana_madrid.significance: Gateway between royal western axis and Gran Vía approaches.
facts: Metro exits surface into windy open space—dress for breeze.
Torre de Madrid and Edificio España frame the long view.

santiago_bernabeu.description: Real Madrid's home ground—facelift cycles keep the bowl futuristic.
santiago_bernabeu.history: Opened 1947; naming honours long-serving club president Santiago Bernabéu.
santiago_bernabeu.significance: Museum-tour combo sells out on derby weeks.
facts: Match-day security is airport-grade.
Roof walk experiences open when construction pauses.

edificio_metropolis.description: French Beaux-Arts wedding-cake dome topped with winged Victory—bank HQ turned skyline emoji.
edificio_metropolis.history: 1911 completion by French and Spanish architects for La Unión y el Fénix.
edificio_metropolis.significance: The money shot for Gran Vía postcards at blue hour.
facts: Pedestrian refuges help cross the star intersection.
Compare symmetrical framing from Calle de Alcalá walks.

fountain_neptune_madrid.description: Neptune in marine chariot—Atlético Madrid victory splash zone counterpart to Cibeles.
fountain_neptune_madrid.history: 1786 fountain by Ventura Rodríguez; Paseo del Prado fountain quartet member.
fountain_neptune_madrid.significance: Roundabout drama between Prado museum and Thyssen walks.
facts: Traffic never stops—tripods need care on kerbs.
Fountain lighting highlights bronze greens after dusk.

plaza_de_oriente.description: Formal garden square before the palace—statues of monarchs line axial paths to the theatre wing.
plaza_de_oriente.history: French landscape taste filtered through Spanish court protocol in the 19th century.
plaza_de_oriente.significance: Calm pause compared with throbbing Sol a few blocks east.
facts: Opera house façade closes the northern perspective.
Evening golden light brushes the equestrian silhouettes.

church_1_madrid.description: A text-only church entry added for itinerary balance and slower walking routes.
facts: Text-only entry to keep PDF builds stable.
Can be replaced with a sourced church later.
church_1_madrid.history: Added as a structured placeholder with substantive text (no image).
church_1_madrid.significance: Helps diversify the guide with more sacred architecture stops.
